title: Open Tibial Fracture - Gustilo IIIB
chief_complaint: Motorcycle accident. My leg bone is sticking out through my skin. There's dirt and debris in the wound. I can see the bone.

physical_exam.Left_Leg: Open fracture of mid-shaft tibia with 8cm wound. Bone exposed and contaminated with road debris and dirt. Periosteal stripping present. No plantar sensation (posterior tibial nerve?). Dorsalis pedis pulse dopplerable. Capillary refill 3 seconds.
physical_exam.ATLS: Primary survey: A patent, B clear, C tachycardic but stable, D alert. No other injuries on secondary survey.

hidden_data.xray: Comminuted displaced mid-shaft tibial fracture. Fibula fractured at same level. Segmental bone loss of 4cm.
hidden_data.ct_angio: 3-vessel runoff to foot (peroneal, anterior tibial, posterior tibial patent). No vascular injury requiring repair.

correct_diagnosis: Open Tibial Shaft Fracture - Gustilo-Anderson Type IIIB (Extensive Soft Tissue Loss with Periosteal Stripping and Contamination)
key_learning_points: Gustilo-Anderson classification: I (<1cm, clean), II (1-10cm, moderate soft tissue injury), IIIA (>10cm, adequate soft tissue coverage), IIIB (extensive periosteal stripping, requires flap coverage), IIIC (vascular injury requiring repair)

Management: 1) ATLS primary survey. 2) Antibiotics within 1h: Cefazolin 2g IV (Gram-positive coverage). ADD Gentamicin 5mg/kg for Type III (Gram-negative). ADD Penicillin 4 million units IV if farm/soil contamination (Clostridium - gas gangrene). 3) Tetanus prophylaxis. 4) Irrigate and debride in OR within 6-24h (remove all devitalized tissue, copious irrigation 9L NS). 5) Stabilize fracture (external fixator initially, convert to intramedullary nail or plate when soft tissue healed)

Serial debridement q24-48h until wound clean. Soft tissue coverage within 5-7 days (local flap, free flap - latissimus dorsi, rectus abdominis, anterolateral thigh). Bone grafting for bone loss (6-12 weeks after soft tissue healed). Complications: Infection (5-15%), Nonunion, Osteomyelitis, Compartment syndrome

Amputation vs salvage: Mangled Extremity Severity Score (MESS) ≥7 predicts need for amputation. Limb salvage may result in multiple surgeries, prolonged disability, and poor functional outcome vs early prosthesis
